QF.id: Introduction to AML, CFT and Proliferation Financing_15

question.text: Which of the following types of reports does FIU-IND receive from reporting entities?

answer.text: Only Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs) and Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs)
Only Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) and Cross Border Wire Transfer Reports (CBWTRs)
Only Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs), STRs, and Non-Profit Organisation Transaction Reports (NTRs)
CTRs, STRs, NTRs, CBWTRs, and Immovable Property Reports (IPRs)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** FIU-IND receives five types of reports: Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs), Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs), Non-Profit Organisation Transaction Reports (NTRs), Cross Border Wire Transfer Reports (CBWTRs), and Reports on Purchase or Sale of Immovable Property (IPRs).
**The Trap:** Options A, B, and C each list only a subset of the reports. The exam may test whether you know the complete list. Remember the acronym: CTR-STR-NTR-CBWTR-IPR.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM III-A and III-C, the focus is typically on CTRs and STRs for securities intermediaries. NISM XXIV expects you to know ALL five report types since it covers the entire AML ecosystem.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** As a securities market intermediary, your primary reporting obligations are CTRs (for cash transactions above Rs. 10 lakh), STRs (for any suspicious transactions), and CBWTRs (for cross-border wire transfers above Rs. 5 lakh).
